subject: Provide a way to trigger Drools rules when an update occurs
commit_message:
Provide a way to trigger Drools rules when an update occurs

An instance of 'DroolsInitFeature.Init' is inserted into a Drools
session when it is initialized, updated, or restored from
persistent data. This provides an opportunity to catch more
initialization scenarios than you can with an empty 'when' clause.

Updates:

1) Change 'DroolsInitFeature' to use Policy Engine scheduled executor

2) Add new test to 'DroolsInitFeatureTest' to verify that the 'Init'
   object is inserted into and deleted from Drools memory

3) Remove trailing spaces

Issue-ID: POLICY-1948
Change-Id: I4748c2415c4563e98b4a53d573c9ebe1e96e8e34
Signed-off-by: Straubs, Ralph (rs8887) <rs8887@att.com>

Patch Set 3:

> (1 comment)

I think if we remove the final to make it easy in junits, I think we could probably go all the way, so we are weakening the constraint, but still imposing not being null at any given time.

Patch Set 3:

> > (1 comment)
 > 
 > I think if we remove the final to make it easy in junits, I think
 > we could probably go all the way, so we are weakening the
 > constraint, but still imposing not being null at any given time.

That's fine.  However, we cannot impose "NotNull", because it may be null, depending on which junit test class is run and in what order.  In fact, I think it's null if this particular test is run by itself.

Patch Set 3:

> > > (1 comment)

 > 
 > That's fine.  However, we cannot impose "NotNull", because it may
 > be null, depending on which junit test class is run and in what
 > order.  In fact, I think it's null if this particular test is run
 > by itself.

couldn't junits simply set a do nothing "NullPolicyEngine" or any implementation anybody wants, o via reflection set it explicitly to null (I think what's the Whitebox statement from Ralph probably uses reflection).

In production code, we always want to maintain the invariant that there is always a non-null PolicyEgine implemention.   If it's open with a setter for generic junit support, the @NonNull annotation in the method should enforce that.   Provided that anybody can use reflection to modify it, but is not part of the contract.

Patch Set 3:

You know, we don't need to remove the "final" modifier.  Just change the junit to insert its own executor into the current manager (and then restore it when done).  That's how the SimpleLockManagerTest does it.

Patch Set 3:

> You know, we don't need to remove the "final" modifier.  Just
 > change the junit to insert its own executor into the current
 > manager (and then restore it when done).  That's how the
 > SimpleLockManagerTest does it.

Yeah, I've seen you doing it on a variety of ways, just wanted to move the review along, may be Ralph could mimic your code in the lock manager then (?) if we agree that the "final" should stay.
